memoryArtifacts.headline: Park Hall
memoryArtifacts.description: Descent: Sir John Botetourt, kt. sold 1373 to Sir Henry de Arden (d. c.1400), kt.; to son, Sir Ralph Arden (d. 1420), kt.; to son, Robert de Arden (d. 1452); to son, Walter Arden (d. 1502); to son, Sir John Arden (d. 1526), kt.; to son, Thomas Arden (d. 1563); to grandson, Edward Arden (d. 1583); forfeited to Crown and granted 1586 to Edward Darcy but in 1609 surrendered and returned to Robert Arden (d. 1635); to grandson, Robert Arden (d. 1643); to four sisters, from whom it was bought up by the husband of Godith Arden, Sir Herbert Price (fl. 1664); to son, John Price (fl. 1704); sold 1704 to John Bridgeman of Castle Bromwich Hall, with which it descended thereafter.

French quotes Walter Arden's will in _Shakspeareana Genealogica_:

“IN THE NAME OF O'r LORD GOD Amen, the last
day of the moneth of Juyll in the yer of our Lord God
m'o vij(c).(1) and in the yere and reigne of Kinge Henry
the vij't the xvij. I Water Arderne Esquire sike in my
body as Neverthelesse I have sufficiently my minde
make this my p'sent Testament and last Will in man'
and forme followinge, First I bequeth my soule to Al-
myghtie God the Holy Trinity, the fader, Sonne, and
Holy Ghost, and oure Lady Saint Marye moder crist,
and to all the Holy companye of Hivn, and my body
to be buryed in the Churche of Saint Peter and Paule
of Aston beside Byrmyngh'm, Also I bequeath to the
Vicar of Aston for my mortuarye any thithes forgotin
my best Oxe, and to the Cathedrall church of Coventr'
and light iij's. iiij'd. and that att the tyme of my buriall be
made vj. torches and xij lb. waxes to be made in light,
And vj. poore men to have vj. blak guones to bere the
said vj. torchies, this to be doon w't part of xx'te marks
that was awarded to be paid to me by my Sonne John
for my buriell, And the residue therof to be disposed by
myn Executrix at myn exequies for my soule and all
cristin soules, And I will also that a Trentall of Masses
be doon for my soule, the soules of my fader and moder
and all cristin soules, And I will that my sonne Thomas
have dureing his lief x marcs whiche I have givin to him.
And that my sonne Martyn have the Maner of Natfeld
dureing his lief according as I therof made hym estate yf
it canne be recorded, And if not thenne I will that the
same Martyn and eny of my other sonnes Rob't Henry
and William have eche of theym v m'rcs by yer duryng

(1) A. D. 1502.

eche of ther lifes. And that my feoffees of my landes make
eche of them a sufficient astate of landes and tenements
to the yearely value of v m'rcs duryng eche of ther lifs.
And I will that my Wife Elianore have the Man' and
place of the Logge and all Landes medewes pastures
and Woods thereto lying duryng her lief. And that my
feoffees thereof make to hir a sufficient astate duryng
the same time. And I geve to the same my Wife all
my goods and Cattells. And the same Elianore my
Wife of this p'sent Testament and last Will I make my
soole Executrix, and Edward Besknap and John Bery-
brigge Squires and John Butteler of Solyhull to ou'see
this my Testament and last Will to be p'formed. These
Witnesseth Richard Styeke Vicar of Aston, John Charnell
and Thomas Ardern Squires, Richard Wright Prest, John
Styeke Prest, Henry Badeley, Richard Flecher, William
Waleyce, Thomas Sandland, Thomas Bull, and other, the
day and yere aboue said. Sealed with my seal.”

“Probatum suit sup' scriptum testamentum apud Lambith.
Extracted from the principal Registry of Her Majesty's Court
of Probate. In the Prerogative Court of Canterbury.”

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter De Arden於1441年出生在英國瓦維其可爾帕可哈爾時，他的父親Robert de Arden I20歲，母親Elizabeth de Clodshale15歲。他於1457年與Eleanor Hampden在英國瓦維其可爾帕可哈爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1502年8月5日在英國瓦維其可爾阿斯頓 塔 比米加姆享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾阿斯頓 塔 比米加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Arden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, either from the district of Arden in Warwickshire or from Arden in Bredbury (Cheshire). The first placename is thought to be derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘high place’, cognate with Ardennes, the name of a forested region on the borders between northeastern France and eastern Belgium. The second derives from Old English hēah ‘high’ + worthign ‘enclosed settlement’.

variants.name: Harden
meanings: English: variant of Arden with prosthetic H-.

English: habitational name, either from Haredene Wood in East Tisbury (Wiltshire), or from Haredon (in Ugborough, Devon). The first placename probably derives from Old English hara ‘hare’ + denu ‘valley’. The second is from Old English hara + dūn ‘hare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter De Arden was born in 1441, in Parkhall, Warwickshire, England, his father, Robert de Arden I, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth de Clodshale, was 15. He married Eleanor Hampden in 1457, in Parkhall, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 5 August 1502, in Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Eleanor Hampden en 1457, à Parkhall, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 5 août 1502, à Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1457 in Parkhall, Warwickshire, England Eleanor Hampden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 5. August 1502 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, England und wurde in Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Eleanor Hampden nel 1457, nel Parkhall, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 5 agosto 1502, nel Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.Church of St Peter/Paul, Aston Juxta Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 애스턴 저스타 비밍함
ko.lifeSummary: Walter De Arden(이)가 1441년 영국 워윅실르 박홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Arden I(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Clodshale(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1457년 영국 워윅실르 박홀에서 Eleanor Hampden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1502년 8월 5일 영국 워윅실르 애스턴 저스타 비밍함에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 애스턴 저스타 비밍함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter De Arden nasceu em 1441, em Parkhall, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Arden I, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Clodshale, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor Hampden em 1457, em Parkhall, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 5 de agosto de 1502, em Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Church of St Peter/Paul, Aston Juxta Eng: астон джукста бирмингхам, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter De Arden родился в 1441 г. в местности пархолл, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Arden I было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Clodshale, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Hampden в 1457 г. в местности пархолл, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 5 августа 1502 г. в местности астон джукста бирмингхам, варвиккшир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности астон джукста бирмингхам, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Church of St Peter/Paul, Aston Juxta Eng: Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter De Arden nació en 1441, en Parkhall, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Arden I, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Clodshale, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Eleanor Hampden en 1457, en Parkhall, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de agosto de 1502, en Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Aston juxta Birmingham, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
